title: Private Banks in Indonesia, Given Risk Profile, Good Corporate Governance, Earnings, and Capital
author: Didin Fatihudin,
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Banks are economic driving institutions; banks with government management and private parties have a role in financial business. This research evaluates the health level of private banks in Indonesia with a sample of Bank BCA, CIMB Niaga, Danamon, and Permata in the 2019-2023 period using a quantitative approach and descriptive method using RGEC (Risk Profile, GCG, Earnings, Capital). The research objective is to assess the bank's financial performance and stability based on four main components. The results show that Bank BCA is consistently "Very Healthy," CIMB Niaga and Danamon have experienced significant increases, while Bank Permata is stable in the "Healthy" category. These findings reflect relatively good financial stability during the study period.

These findings reflect relatively good financial stability during the study period.
keywords: Private Bank; Risk Profile; Good Corporate Governance; Earnings; Capital
